message:
perf: reduce allocations across agent core, model catalog, and tool output paths

A sweep of allocation-reduction changes touching several hot paths:

Agent event dispatch (agent.rs, interactive/agent.rs):
- Replace Box<AssistantMessageEvent> with inline enum in AgentEvent::MessageUpdate,
  eliminating a heap allocation on every streaming token/chunk event. The enum is
  already small enough that boxing provided no benefit.
- Switch session_id fields from String to Arc<str> to avoid per-event cloning of
  the session identifier across AgentStart/AgentEnd/TurnStart/TurnEnd variants.
- Use std::mem::take instead of .drain(..).collect::<Vec<_>>() when flushing the
  UI stream delta batcher pending buffer, avoiding an intermediate Vec allocation.

Interactive message dispatch (interactive.rs):
- Use the new try_downcast() method (from charmed_rust) instead of
  downcast_ref + clone for PiMsg handling, eliminating a per-token clone
  of every PiMsg variant in the update hot path.

Model catalog (models.rs):
- Add CRC32C-keyed disk cache for the parsed legacy model catalog
  (LEGACY_MODELS_GENERATED_TS), persisted to ~/.cache/pi/models-cache/.
  The embedded TypeScript catalog is ~large and parsing it on every startup
  was measurable. Cache is content-addressed by CRC32C of the source text,
  so it self-invalidates on any catalog change.
- Cache the legacy_provider_ids() HashSet in a OnceLock instead of
  recomputing it on every call.
- Cache parsed Api enum strings in built_in_models() to avoid redundant
  parse + to_string cycles for repeated API identifiers.
- Avoid redundant to_ascii_lowercase() calls in the upstream model
  dedup loop by computing it once and reusing.
- Derive Serialize on CompatConfig and LegacyGeneratedModel to support
  the disk cache serialization.

Provider suggestions (providers/mod.rs):
- Replace sort_by_key (which clones the name String for each comparison)
  with sort_by using direct field references.

Tool output (tools.rs):
- Replace truncation.content.clone() with std::mem::take() in ReadTool,
  run_bash_command, GrepTool, FindTool, and LsTool output paths. The
  truncation struct is not used after the content is extracted, so moving
  the String out avoids copying potentially large output buffers.

CLI output (main.rs):
- Buffer list_providers() output through BufWriter<Stdout> to collapse
  per-row write syscalls into a single flush.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
